patch.value: pm create <type> --title X silently ignores positional type, defaults to Task
pm create feature --title 'x' produces a Feature (not Task); positional type honored whether title is positional or via --title flag

## Repro (pm 2026.6.13 build)
```
pm create feature --title 'x' --json   # => item.type == 'Task'  (BUG: positional 'feature' dropped)
pm create Feature 'x' --json           # => item.type == 'Feature' (positional title path OK)
pm create --type Feature --title 'x'   # => item.type == 'Feature' (explicit flag OK)
```
When a leading positional type name is given together with the `--title` flag, the positional type is ignored and create falls back to the default type (Task). Only the `<type> <title>` all-positional form or the explicit `--type` flag are honored.

## Expected
A leading positional that matches a known/registered type should set the item type regardless of whether the title arrives positionally or via `--title`. Otherwise emit a usage error rather than silently mistyping (agent footgun — see closed pm-edge for the create-type mistype class).

Discovered during the GH-210/GH-212 validate work (pm-gnnb) while seeding sandbox fixtures.
